problem: The desk loop can publish market context from stale upstream snapshots when data pipelines partially fail; there is no explicit SLA gate that blocks publication if snapshot age exceeds threshold.

evidence.symptom: Research decisions risk being made on outdated context without a hard freshness stop.

required_change: Define a hard freshness SLA for MC inputs (e.g., max snapshot age) and expose it as config.
Fail closed in MC brief generation when any required input exceeds SLA, returning explicit stale-data reason codes.
Emit machine-readable freshness metadata in every MC brief artifact (input timestamps, max_age_seconds, pass/fail).
Add operator-facing guidance in output for stale-data recovery steps.
acceptance_tests: Unit test: stale input timestamps trigger fail-closed status and non-zero exit path.
Unit test: fresh inputs pass and include freshness metadata block.
Regression test: partial-data mode cannot bypass stale-data guard.
Manual proof: one fresh run artifact and one stale simulated run artifact with expected statuses.
constraints: Do not relax existing risk gates or sizing logic.
No silent fallback to stale data under any mode.
Maintain backward-compatible JSON keys where possible; additive metadata preferred.
